mission.title: Install and Configure Tokenetes on Kubernetes
mission.description: Tokenetes is an open source Transaction Tokens (TraTs) Service — an implementation of the IETF OAuth Transaction Tokens draft specification. It enables secure propagation of identity and context across microservices via transaction tokens. Tokenetes uses SPIRE for workload identity and requires tconfigd for managing TraT configurations.

steps.title: Install tconfigd (prerequisite)
steps.description:
Tokenetes requires tconfigd to be installed first. tconfigd manages Transaction Token (TraT) configurations as Kubernetes custom resources. Clone and install from the separate tconfigd repository:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/tokenetes/tconfigd.git
cd tconfigd
```
Follow the tconfigd README for installation. tconfigd must be running in the `tokenetes-system` namespace before deploying the Tokenetes service.

steps.title: Clone the Tokenetes repository
steps.description:
Clone the Tokenetes repository which contains the Kubernetes manifests:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/tokenetes/tokenetes.git
cd tokenetes
```

steps.title: Configure the Tokenetes manifests
steps.description:
Before deploying, update the Kubernetes manifest files in the `kubernetes/` directory with your environment-specific values:

1. Replace `[your-namespace]` with your target namespace in all YAML files
2. Replace `[your-trust-domain]` with your SPIRE trust domain
3. Update the SPIRE agent socket path in `tokenetes-deployment.yaml` if your SPIRE agent socket is not at the default location (`/run/spire/sockets`)

```bash
# Create the target namespace
kubectl create namespace <your-namespace>
```

steps.title: Deploy the Tokenetes service
steps.description:
Apply the Kubernetes manifests from the `kubernetes/` directory in order:
```bash
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service-account.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-deployment.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Confirm that tconfigd is running in the tokenetes-system namespace and the Tokenetes service pods are running in your application namespace:
```bash
# Check tconfigd
kubectl get pod -n tokenetes-system

# Check Tokenetes service
kubectl get pod -n <your-namespace> | grep tokenetes

# Check all deployments and services
kubectl get deployments,svc -n <your-namespace>
```

steps.title: Verify TraT configuration
steps.description:
List and inspect the deployed Transaction Token (TraT) custom resources:
```bash
# List all TraTs in your namespace
kubectl get trats -n <your-namespace>

# Describe a specific TraT for details
kubectl describe trats/<trat-name> -n <your-namespace>
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show tconfigd running in the tokenetes-system namespace and Tokenetes service pods running in your application namespace. You can verify TraT configurations with `kubectl get trats -n <your-namespace>`.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service-account.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-deployment.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service.yaml

kubectl get pod -n tokenetes-system
kubectl get pod -n <your-namespace> | grep tokenetes

uninstall.title: Delete Tokenetes resources
uninstall.description:
Remove the Tokenetes deployment, service, and service account from your namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service.yaml
kubectl delete -f kubernetes/tokenetes-deployment.yaml
kubectl delete -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service-account.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and TraT resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Transaction Token custom resources and CRDs that were created:
```bash
kubectl delete trats --all -n <your-namespace>
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep tokenetes | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
If you created a dedicated namespace for Tokenetes, remove it:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace <your-namespace>
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Pull the latest manifests
upgrade.description:
Update your local clone of the Tokenetes repository to get the latest Kubernetes manifests:
```bash
cd tokenetes
git pull origin main
```

upgrade.title: Re-apply the manifests
upgrade.description:
After updating the manifests with your environment-specific values, re-apply them:
```bash
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service-account.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-deployment.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Confirm the updated pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n <your-namespace>
kubectl describe deployment tokenetes -n <your-namespace>
```

troubleshooting.title: tconfigd not running
troubleshooting.description:
Tokenetes requires tconfigd to be installed and running. Verify it is in the tokenetes-system namespace:
```bash
kubectl get pod -n tokenetes-system
kubectl logs <tconfigd-pod-name> -n tokenetes-system
```
If tconfigd is not running, install it from https://github.com/tokenetes/tconfigd before deploying the Tokenetes service.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Tokenetes pods are in CrashLoopBackOff, check the logs for configuration issues:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n <your-namespace>
```
Common causes include: missing or incorrect SPIRE agent socket path, tconfigd not running, or incorrect trust domain configuration.

troubleshooting.title: SPIRE agent socket not found
troubleshooting.description:
If logs show errors about the SPIRE agent socket, verify the hostPath in the deployment matches your SPIRE agent socket location:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment tokenetes -n <your-namespace>
```
The default path is `/run/spire/sockets`. Update the `spire-agent-socket` volume's `hostPath.path` in `tokenetes-deployment.yaml` if your setup differs.

troubleshooting.title: TraT resources not created
troubleshooting.description:
If `kubectl get trats` returns no resources, ensure tconfigd is running and the TraT CRD is installed:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep tokenetes
kubectl get pod -n tokenetes-system
```
If the CRD is missing, tconfigd may not have been installed correctly.

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher with SPIRE installed for workload identity. kubectl and git must be installed on your local machine. tconfigd must be deployed (from https://github.com/tokenetes/tconfigd) before installing the Tokenetes service.
